symptoms.symptom_name: Severe orthopnoea - drowning sensation lying flat, sits bolt upright on three pillows

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe orthopnoea is relatively specific for raised left-heart filling pressures and pulmonary congestion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea - woke gasping for air

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: PND is a hallmark of decompensated heart failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Massive bilateral pitting oedema to the thighs/sacrum with 5 kg rapid weight gain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid weight gain and massive dependent oedema objectively mark fluid overload severity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Missed three days of furosemide (refill error) - reversible precipitant

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Abrupt cessation of diuresis is a classic, reversible precipitant of acute decompensation.
Absence of fever and purulent sputum argues against pneumonia as the cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated JVP (8 cm), S3 gallop, displaced apex, hepatomegaly with positive hepatojugular reflux, and bibasal crackles to mid-zones

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Raised JVP with an S3 and bibasal crackles is the classic examination signature of biventricular congestion/pulmonary oedema.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated BNP/NT-proBNP with congestion on investigation

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated natriuretic peptides consistent with heart failure decompensation, with echocardiographic impairment of cardiac function
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severity is not quantified and underlying cardiac function is uncharacterised.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Elevated natriuretic peptides support heart-failure decompensation.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute decompensated heart failure with pulmonary oedema
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Severe orthopnoea, PND, massive oedema/rapid weight gain and a missed-diuretic precipitant, with resting hypoxaemia, together establish acute pulmonary oedema requiring urgent potent diuresis, oxygen and monitoring.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Supports the diagnosis, gauges severity, and guides safe diuresis (electrolytes/renal function).
ECG for arrhythmia/ischaemia as a precipitant; chest X-ray to confirm pulmonary oedema and exclude alternatives.
Assesses oxygenation and ventilation, informing the need for non-invasive ventilation.
Characterises cardiac function once stabilised to guide ongoing heart-failure therapy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Only restarting the missed oral diuretic and waiting for the routine GP round
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Insufficient alone for acute pulmonary oedema with resting hypoxaemia, which needs urgent potent (IV) diuresis, oxygen and monitoring.
